chunks.text: The UC Berkeley Women in Computer Science and Engineering (WiCSE) 40th Anniversary Reunion, which celebrates 40 years of women in technology at Berkeley, will be held on Saturday, March 17, 2018. Join us for a day packed full of reunions, enlightening talks, and networking opportunities! A note from WiCSE Co-Presidents Alice Ye and Coline Devin: We are proud to add speakers throughout the day including Prof. Tsu-Jae King Liu. Prof. King Liu was the Chair of the Electrical Engineering and Computer Science from July 2014-June 2016, and is the current Vice-Provost for Academic and Space Planning at University of California, Berkeley.

WiCSE is also planning a day of activities including lightning talks on career paths and research by current graduate students and alumni, retrospectives on WiCSE history, as well as panels and discussions on diversity within electrical engineering and computer science. We hope this event will foster mentorship and visibility for alumnae and current students.
Date and Time: Saturday, March 17, 2018, 11:00 AM – 6:00 PM PDT. Location: Banatao Auditorium and Kvamme Atrium, 3rd floor Sutardja Dai Hall, 1860 Leroy Avenue, Berkeley, CA 94720.

Sessions: Technical Lightning Talks from Alumnae, Women in Tech 'Pathways after Berkeley' Panel, Diversity Round Table – what has worked and what hasn’t to include more women in Tech, Perspectives through the Decades of WICSE.

Lightning Speakers: Marti Hearst, EECS/iSchool, UC Berkeley; Lili Kan, Uber Advanced Technology Group; Gireeja Ranade, Microsoft Research AI; Virginia Smith, Stanford/CMU; Beth Trushkowsky, Harvey Mudd College; Ei-Mang Wu, TenMarks.
Keynote Speakers: Diane Greene, CEO of Google Cloud; Tsu-Jae King Liu, EECS Professor at UC Berkeley; Barbara Simons, Board Chair of Verified Voting.

Event FAQ: Transportation/parking options include street parking on Hearst Ave or public transportation from Downtown Berkeley BART Station. Contact wicse.berkeley@gmail.com for questions. For sponsorship inquiries, contact Becca Roelofs (roelofs at cs.berkeley.edu).

Panelists: Pathways After Berkeley includes Adrienne Felt (Google Chrome), Leslie Field (Ice911 Research), Janey Hoe (Cisco Investments), Hayley Iben (Pixar). Diversity Round Table includes Francesca Barrientos (Cisco), Chen-Nee Chuah (UC Davis), Regina Eckert (Bias Busters), Marie-Ange Eyoum Tagne (Amazon). Perspectives Through the Decades includes Paula Hawthorn (retired), Kim Keeton (HP Labs), Isabelle Stanton (Google).

Women Faculty in EE: Ana Claudia Arias, Connie Chang-Hasnain, Tsu-Jae King Liu, Rikky Muller, Gireeja Ranade, Somayeh Sojoudi, Alexandra von Meier, Avideh Zakhor. Women Faculty in EE/CS: Ruzena Bajcsy, Claire Tomlin, Laura Waller, Bin Yu. Women Faculty in CS: Anca Dragan, Shafi Goldwasser, Sue Graham (emerita), Marti Hearst, Jennifer Listgarten, Raluca Ada Popa, Sylvia Ratnasamy, Dawn Song, Kathy Yelick.

Quotes: Tsu-Jae King Liu invites attendees to reconnect and meet new generations. Sheila Humphreys emphasizes the importance of alumnae in sustaining the WiCSE community and welcomes them to celebrate on March 17.

Planning Committee: Chairs: Alice Ye and Coline Devin; Promotions Designer: Urmita Sikder; Guest Coordinator: Carolyn Chen; Industry/Funding Coordinator: Becca Roelofs; Speaker Coordinator: Elizabeth Yang; Secretary: Alyssa Morrow; Advisor: Sheila Humphreys; EECS Staff: Jenny Jones, Audrey Sillers, Josephine Williamson.
